response.justification: The candidate is a strong hands-on generalist with roughly 19 years of experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, which satisfies the tenure requirement. However, the role is a director-level IC focused on AI strategy, and the resume shows only recent, tooling-level AI exposure (MCP code generators, agent infrastructure since 2025) rather than recognized AI expertise, shipped AI products or platforms, or advanced AI education. There is also little evidence of the strategic, executive-facing scope expected at principal level — advising strategic customers, shaping product direction, or influencing at organizational scale.
strengths: 19+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency distributed systems
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of an open-source geo-data platform with real-world adoption
Active knowledge-sharing through conference talks, workshops, and open-source libraries
concerns: AI experience is recent and tooling-focused, far short of the 'recognized AI expertise' and demonstrated AI products the role prefers
Career trajectory tops out at senior/staff IC roles with no evidence of director-level strategic or advisory scope
No advanced degree in an AI-related field and limited cloud platform strategy exposure beyond project-level AWS use

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise and AI product track record
Seniority gap versus director-level strategic scope
Long, broad engineering tenure with entrepreneurial experience
